subject: Sociology
title: Neoliberalism
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Neoliberalism.

hook.text: Free markets, low taxes, privatization, deregulation. These ideas reshaped the world — and not equally.

buildup.text: Neoliberalism promotes free markets, minimal state intervention, and private enterprise as the path to prosperity.

discovery.text: Since the 1980s, neoliberal policies spread globally through institutions like the IMF, World Bank, and WTO.

twist.text: Critics argue neoliberalism widened inequality, weakened public services, and shifted risk from states to individuals.

climax.text: Neoliberalism isn't just economics. It's an ideology that turns citizens into consumers and society into a market.

punchline.text: The market became the answer to everything.

quiz.question: What does neoliberalism promote?
options: Free markets, privatization, and minimal state intervention
